mechanism: The fiscal deficit keeps the economy near potential, preventing the output gap from opening sufficiently for last-mile disinflation. Simultaneously, the NKPC convexity near potential means disinflation requires a sacrifice ratio of 4-5x, which the fiscal floor renders impossible. This is the Sargent-Wallace fiscal dominance problem.
regime_dependence: Holds when core PCE is above target (currently ~2.5-3%) and the deficit maintains the economy near potential. A favorable supply shock could break the trap without demand destruction.
